description: Alien and bioengineered plants from science fiction settings.
plants: Chromatic Spire
A crystalline plant that refracts light into shifting rainbows, growing in spiraling towers on low-gravity worlds.
Synth-Moss
Engineered moss that scrubs carbon dioxide from station air, spreading across walls in programmed geometric patterns.
Plasma Vine
A plant that channels ambient electrical energy through its veins, crackling faintly in the dark.
Void Orchid
A flower that blooms in hard vacuum, its petals a membrane that photosynthesizes starlight.
Terraform Seedling
A fast-growing engineered tree designed to convert alien soil, the first step in making a dead world breathable.
Neural Fungus
A bioluminescent network of mycelium that responds to thought, pulsing brighter when sentient minds are near.
Gravity Bloom
A floating plant that hovers above the ground, its root tendrils dangling in the air, feeding on moisture and dust.
Xenofern
Alien fronds that curl and uncurl based on atmospheric pressure, serving as living barometers.
Carbon Lattice Tree
A tree-like structure with a trunk of woven carbon fiber, grown rather than manufactured.
Cryo-Lily
A flower that thrives in sub-zero temperatures, its petals made of thin ice crystals that never melt.
Spore Tower
A tall fungal structure that periodically releases clouds of spores, spreading its colony across entire continents.
Dataroot
A bioengineered plant whose root network stores and transmits data, a living alternative to fiber optic cable.
